name: Hao-Chun (Oscar) Shih
summary: Data Science graduate student at the University of Michigan (GPA: 4.00), skilled in machine learning, NLP, analytics, database systems, and quantitative modeling. Experienced across the full data science stack from ETL pipelines to production ML systems. Published researcher at EMNLP with industry experience at Micron Technology, Academia Sinica, and WPG Holdings.

education.degree: Bachelor of Science in Applied Mathematics

experience.title: Data Analyst Intern
highlights: Built burn-in board dashboard end-to-end including data pipeline re-engineering with Python automation and SQL query optimization, improving machine allocation accuracy by 16% and enabling real-time anomaly detection for cross-team monitoring

Redesigned 3 operational dashboards by resolving join logic inconsistencies and schema conflicts through cross-platform data validation (SQL Server, TSUM, Tableau), collaborating with 10+ teams to standardize metric definitions and establish single source of truth

Developed demand forecasting solution using ensemble methods (Random Forest, XGBoost) and deep learning (LSTM & attention) on 50K+ time-series records, reducing forecast error (MAPE) by 43% for monthly capacity planning

experience.title: Research Assistant
highlights: Analyzed performance and cost trade-offs in an enterprise internal knowledge retrieval system (E-Sun Bank internal chatbot) by evaluating domain-specific LLM customization strategies with PEFT techniques including LoRA, Adapter, Parallel Adapter, Mix-and-Match Adapter, and Prefix Tuning
Designed and executed 60+ A/B tests to assess the impact of model configuration, training checkpoints, and data variations on retrieval quality and response consistency
Delivered a PEFT and RAG-integrated prototype with clear recommendations that improved retrieval F1 by 10% and supported an estimated 4% productivity gain for enterprise users
Presented research results at the internationally recognized EMNLP conference

experience.title: Business Intelligence Intern
highlights: Re-engineered legacy DataStage ETL pipelines by refactoring join logic to eliminate nested loop operations and duplicate record generation, accelerating Cognos report loading time by 80%

Led migration of 54 scheduled DataStage jobs to SSIS through systematic reverse-engineering and selective optimization, reducing average processing time by 13% to enable Oracle ERP integration and multi-region data consolidation
Streamlined 27 Cognos electronic forms across accounting, sales, and procurement functions by resolving backend integration issues, enhancing data reliability and operational efficiency
Strengthened reporting system stability by addressing ETL failure patterns and performance bottlenecks, driving $50K in annual cost savings through reduced maintenance overhead

projects.name: JPMorgan ML: Income Classification & Customer Segmentation
highlights: Built end-to-end ML workflow for financial services analytics combining income classification with customer segmentation
Evaluated multiple classification models using ROC-AUC, PR-AUC, threshold tuning, and confusion matrix trade-offs
Discovered interpretable customer segments through dimensionality reduction and clustering
Profiled each segment by income potential, employment patterns, education, and demographic characteristics to support strategic decision-making

projects.name: GUI-ASFormer: Transformer-Based GUI Video Segmentation
highlights: Built end-to-end video segmentation pipeline on 2,000+ GUI interaction videos to model user action sequences from high-dimensional, unstructured visual data
Fine-tuned temporal models and designed a Swin Transformer with spatial attention to improve representation learning for high-resolution GUI environments
Detected keyframes from minimal visual changes and improved segment accuracy with post-refinement
Achieved 38% accuracy improvement; enables downstream use cases like GUI agent training and tutorial summarization

projects.name: Health Activity Recommender: Contextual Bandit for Personalized Wellness
highlights: Developed reinforcement learning-based recommendation system to personalize health activity suggestions using historical engagement signals and user context
Applied contextual multi-armed bandit via Thompson Sampling to balance exploration and exploitation
Boosted user step counts by 15-20% through tailored suggestions
Enhanced recommendation accuracy by 25% through integrating contextual factors like user preferences and health data

projects.name: Unsupervised Learning Core: K-Means, GMM, and CVAE Implementations
highlights: Implemented K-Means from first principles for intuitive cluster separation
Built Gaussian Mixture Models using EM algorithm for soft assignment and probabilistic density estimation
Constructed Conditional Variational Autoencoder (CVAE) to learn latent representations and generate samples conditioned on labels
Visualized clustering and latent space in 2D for real-world datasets including MNIST

projects.name: Transfer Learning: Vision & Language Modeling with Transformers
highlights: Fine-tuned ResNet with limited data for high-accuracy image classification
Built transformer decoder-only model for next-token prediction (GPT-style language generation)
Implemented self-attention and causal masking from scratch
Demonstrated task switching across computer vision and NLP domains

projects.name: CNN for Image Classification & RNN for Image Captioning
highlights: Engineered deep learning pipeline entirely from scratch using NumPy, combining CNN for image feature extraction and RNN for caption generation
Achieved 82% image classification accuracy and BLEU-1 score of 0.52 using mini-batch SGD and Adam optimizer
Enhanced model stability with batch normalization and dropout; validated correctness via gradient checking

projects.name: Facebook-Style Relational Database Design
highlights: Designed ER diagram to model entities such as users, friendships, messages, photos, and events for a Facebook-scale social platform
Implemented SQL scripts to create and manage database objects ensuring referential integrity
Developed views and queries to facilitate data retrieval and analysis at scale

projects.name: Facebook-Style JDBC Database Interaction
highlights: Implemented SQL queries to extract meaningful insights from social network database
Utilized JDBC for database connectivity and data manipulation
Organized code to separate concerns between data access and business logic

projects.name: MongoDB User Data Analysis & Query
highlights: Migrated relational data from Oracle to MongoDB, transforming into JSON document format
Performed queries to analyze friend counts, restore broken friendships, and extract birth month distributions
Applied MongoDB aggregation framework for efficient data grouping and statistical analysis

projects.name: Grace Hash Join Implementation
highlights: Implemented Grace Hash Join algorithm to perform efficient joins on large datasets under memory constraints
Designed partition phase using hash function to divide large relations into disk-based buckets
Handled recursive repartitioning when intermediate partitions exceed available buffers
Simulated buffer pool management and disk I/O to mimic realistic system limitations

projects.name: Tornado Interactive Dashboard
highlights: Built interactive dashboard over 60,000+ tornado events with pre-aggregated metrics and interactive filtering
Supports filtering by state, year, and Fujita scale (EF0-EF5)
Visualizes fatalities, injuries, and property/crop damage through charts and heatmaps
Deployed as live Streamlit app enabling stakeholder self-service exploration

projects.name: Interactive Visualization of School Demographics and Performance
highlights: Explored relationships among school demographics, economic status, and academic performance in New York State
Built scatter + bar chart combo to visualize how financial status relates to scores with linked race composition charts
Developed interactive map with gender pyramid to explore geographic patterns across NYC schools
Designed for educational researchers and policy analysts to gain intuitive insights from large public datasets

projects.name: Health Insurance Cost Prediction
highlights: Analyzed how Age, BMI, smoking status, and number of children influence insurance premiums
Performed feature engineering including Box-Cox transformations for normalizing skewed data
Applied Linear Regression, Polynomial Regression, and Random Forest; conducted ANOVA and VIF analysis to address multicollinearity
Reduced RMSE by 70% in test data, enabling better cost estimations

projects.name: Statistical Inference and Density Estimation
highlights: Estimated t-distribution parameters based on kurtosis for modeling fat tails in financial returns
Applied Method of Moments to fit Gaussian Mixture Models
Applied Kernel Density Estimation (KDE) and minimized Integrated Squared Error (ISE)
Simulated S&P 500 returns and constructed confidence intervals for financial analysis

projects.name: Extreme Value Theory for Financial Risk Estimation
highlights: Applied Extreme Value Theory (EVT) to estimate tail risks in financial markets for modeling rare events like market crashes
Utilized Peaks Over Threshold (POT) method with Generalized Pareto Distribution (GPD) for tail modeling
Compared EVT-based VaR and ES estimates with historical simulation; validated with backtesting procedures

projects.name: Robust Dependence Modeling and Copula Simulation
highlights: Implemented various copula families including Gaussian, T, Clayton, and Gumbel to model complex dependence structures between financial assets
Simulated joint distributions with specified marginal behaviors and analyzed tail dependencies
Improved dependency modeling accuracy by 30%

projects.name: Multivariate Risk Modeling and Portfolio Optimization
highlights: Modeled asset co-movements using multivariate statistics and copulas to capture nonlinear and tail dependencies
Constructed Efficient Frontier identifying optimal portfolios that maximize return for a given level of risk
Evaluated VaR and Expected Shortfall (ES) to measure tail risks
Integrated CAPM to assess expected returns relative to systematic market risk (beta)
Conducted 10,000+ Monte Carlo simulations to derive portfolio-level risk metrics under stress scenarios

publications.title: Optimizing Knowledge Updates with PEFT in Neural Language Models
highlights: Surveyed PEFT architectures including LoRA, Adapter, Parallel Adapter, Mix-and-Match Adapter, and Prefix Tuning
Investigated features of FFN-Integrated and Attention-Integrated modules
Revised evaluation methods on memorization ability of LLMs and quantified knowledge retention
Applied findings to E-Sun Bank enterprise internal chatbot system

structure: Opening: express genuine interest in the role and company, mention 1 specific thing about the company that resonates
Body 1: highlight the most relevant work experience with specific quantified metrics
Body 2: highlight complementary skills from projects or coursework that directly map to the JD requirements
Closing: express enthusiasm, mention availability, thank the reader
emphasize: EMNLP publication and NLP/GenAI research expertise
full-stack data science skills from ETL pipelines to production ML systems
avoid: listing too many technologies without context
mentioning every project — pick 2-3 most relevant ones
